decisions.decision: Keep current SQLite task/artifact/trace/service-request spine; do not replace it with a framework runtime yet.
decisions.why: The lab already has gates, packet schemas, chain integrity, and review artifacts. Replacing it before worker execution starts would add migration risk without earning more money-path throughput.

decisions.decision: Promote Pydantic AI as the first typed worker implementation path for local/non-gated model workflows.
decisions.why: Strong Python fit, provider-agnostic model layer, typed validation, and observability align with existing Pydantic eval artifacts.

decisions.decision: Evaluate LangGraph for stateful manager and seeker loops after current service-worker approval chain is stable.
decisions.why: Durable execution and human-in-the-loop features match manager checkpoints and reviewable state updates.

decisions.decision: Track Microsoft Agent Framework as the likely cross-runtime production target; do not build on AutoGen for new core work.
decisions.why: MAF is the active Microsoft path with production, governance, durability, workflow, MCP/A2A, Python/.NET support; AutoGen is maintenance-mode.

decisions.decision: Keep SQLite queue reports now; next adapter proof should compare DBOS and Hatchet against existing service-worker packet semantics.
decisions.why: DBOS is minimal Postgres-backed durable execution; Hatchet is stronger when queue scale, retries, monitoring, and self-hosted dashboard matter.

decisions.decision: Keep all legal/KYC/payment/wallet/account/browser-public actions as service requests requiring human/CRO decisions.
decisions.why: Every external action class remains a boundary; runtime choice must not bypass approval records or exact-scope review.

next_actions: Add a local runtime-bridge scorecard mapping Microsoft Agent Framework, LangGraph, Pydantic AI, DBOS, Hatchet, and current SQLite reports against the 11 pending service-worker packets.
Build one dry-run Pydantic AI worker only for a local artifact summarization task with api_calls=false unless model/API approval is explicitly granted.
Design a DBOS-vs-Hatchet adapter test that consumes service-worker request packets and emits local result placeholders without enqueueing external work.
Keep AutoGen references only for migration/pattern comparison; remove it from any new-core recommendation unless MAF is unavailable.

sources.evidence: Open multi-language framework for production-grade AI agents and multi-agent workflows in .NET/Python; emphasizes durability, restartability, observability, governance, human-in-the-loop control, graph workflows, MCP/A2A, and hosted/cloud patterns.
sources.name: Microsoft Agent Framework

sources.evidence: AutoGen README now marks the project maintenance mode and points new users to Microsoft Agent Framework; keep it as legacy pattern source, not new core.
sources.name: Microsoft AutoGen

sources.evidence: Low-level infrastructure for long-running stateful workflows/agents with durable execution, human-in-the-loop, memory, and trace/debugging support.
sources.name: LangGraph

sources.evidence: Python agent framework for production-grade GenAI applications/workflows; model-agnostic provider support and OpenTelemetry/Logfire observability fit typed local workers.
sources.name: Pydantic AI

sources.evidence: Orchestration engine for background tasks, AI agents, and durable workflows; supports Python/TypeScript/Go/Ruby with queueing, retries, durability, monitoring, alerting, and logging.
sources.name: Hatchet

sources.evidence: Postgres-backed durable Python workflows and queues; checkpointing/resume, queue concurrency/rate limits, scheduling, notifications, programmatic workflow management.
sources.name: DBOS Transact Python
